summary: High‑energy RFK show with epic Terrapin jam and tight Bird Song
blurb: The set bursts open with a punchy Feel Like A Stranger and rolls through strong renditions of Lazy River Road, Bird Song and a monster second‑set suite that weaves Playing In The Band into an extended Terrapin Station jam. Jerry’s guitar soars on the Terrapin and Days Between jams while Vince provides solid keys despite mixed reactions to Hornsby’s guest accordion. Overall a high‑energy performance with standout jams throughout.
review: June 26, 1993 at RFK Stadium captured one of the Dead’s most exuberant summer outings. The first set kicks off with the bright “Feel Like A Stranger,” then slides into a melodic groove on “Lazy River Road” and a soaring “Bird Song.” The real fireworks arrive in the second set: a blistering “Playing In The Band” segues seamlessly into an almost half‑hour Terrapin Station odyssey, followed by a marathon sequence of drums, Space, “The Last Time,” a monster “Days Between” jam (where Jerry is described as wailing on the magic wand), and a fiery “Throwing Stones.” Vince Welnick’s keyboards lock in tight, though Bruce Hornsby’s guest accordion/piano adds a polarizing flavor that some fans love and others find distracting. Bob Weir’s vocals show a rasp from recent throat surgery, but his grit only adds character. The crowd’s energy never wanes, culminating in a rousing “One More Saturday Night” and an encore of “Liberty.” With crisp SBD capture (aside from occasional mix quirks) this performance stands out as a high‑octane showcase of the 1993 lineup’s improvisational firepower.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise the extended Terrapin jam, Bird Song, Days Between and overall high energy; no consistent negative comments about playing quality, only mixed remarks on Hornsby’s guest. Positive language appears in three of four analyses, yielding a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crisp, well‑balanced soundboard capture that showcases the high‑energy performance and extended jams without major dropouts.

key_highlights: Extended Playing In The Band → Terrapin Station jam (~30‑35 min) that drives the second set.
Monster Days Between/Throwing Stones sequence within a massive second‑set suite.
Tight, melodic performances of Bird Song and Lazy River Road; strong opener with Feel Like A Stranger.

band_performance.Jerry: Ripping solos and intense guitar work, especially on Terrapin Station and the wailing Days Between jam; highlighted by a memorable sit‑in with Sting.
band_performance.Bob: Scratchy vocal tone from recent throat surgery noted but adds character; no major criticism of his playing.
